danger_signals.visual_form: Aircraft radar blips and cityscape alerts in management mode
danger_signals.meaning: Active alien threat requiring interception; collateral damage risk to city infrastructure

danger_signals.visual_form: Tetrahedron-shaped teleport gates manifesting in battlescape
danger_signals.meaning: Direct ground invasion; immediate combat engagement required

danger_signals.visual_form: Weaponized alien creature jumping and latching onto individual unit
danger_signals.meaning: Biological threat; potential infection/infestation of X-COM personnel or civilians

danger_signals.visual_form: Funding withdrawal notification in cityscape interface
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of primary income source; financial crisis imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Active blockades or hostile faction icons in city management display
danger_signals.meaning: Restricted movement, resource denial, or potential armed conflict with corporate entities

danger_signals.visual_form: Corporation control percentage or infected facility markers in cityscape
danger_signals.meaning: Organization compromised by extraterrestrial takeover; strategic asset loss

safety_signals.visual_form: Budget allocation confirmation in management interface
safety_signals.meaning: Financial stability maintained; X-COM operational capacity secured

safety_signals.visual_form: Green or neutral faction relationship indicators in cityscape
safety_signals.meaning: Organization support active; access to resources, personnel transit, or manufacturing intact

safety_signals.visual_form: Destroyed aircraft status update in cityscape; alien artifact recovery notification
safety_signals.meaning: Threat neutralized; salvageable materials secure for research and income generation

safety_signals.visual_form: High weekly battlescape performance ratings; advancing X-COM technology tree
safety_signals.meaning: Operational superiority; capacity to suppress alien advancement or force political leverage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: X-COM units identifiable in battlescape by equipment and armor style
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: X-COM bases and facilities within Mega-Primus cityscape; aircraft hangar locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Government authorization tokens; funding flow indicators in management mode
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Government buildings and administrative sectors in Mega-Primus

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corporate blockade markers; personnel movement restrictions in cityscape
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Transtellar facilities; corporate headquarters locations visually marked

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Religious fanatic iconography; alien worship symbols
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Inherently hostile faction markers; cult member unit models in battlescape
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cult stronghold locations within city; religious iconography in structures

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: 14 distinct alien races visually differentiated: Anthropods, Brainsuckers, Hyperworms, Megaspawns, Micronoids, etc. Each with unique creature model and animation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tetrahedron-shaped teleport gates; alien-infested facilities showing visual corruption or alien biomass

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Marginalized minority unit models; hybrid/android character designs distinct from baseline humans
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hybrid/android residential or cultural enclaves within Mega-Primus; political pressure group headquarters
Mars facility visual distinction; off-world mining infrastructure representation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Military authority uniforms/equipment; security personnel models
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mars colony security checkpoints and fortified positions

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early campaign: Alien threat emerging, government support intact
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple active organizations visible in cityscape; funding indicators positive; few alien markers on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Organizations operate independently; no corporate hostility; UFO encounters infrequent but escalating

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid campaign: Alien infestation spreading across organizations
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Infected facility markers appearing on cityscape; organization control percentages shifting; tetrahedron gate visual presence increases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Compromised corporations withdraw support or actively oppose X-COM; aliens attempt takeovers rather than diplomacy; covert corporate battles intensify

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Government becomes hostile due to alien interference or X-COM collateral damage
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Government funding meter drops to zero; hostile faction indicator displayed; potential raid mission markers appear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No further government income; X-COM must raid corporations to sustain operations; financial stress intensifies

world_state_to_visual.world_state: X-COM achieves military dominance through battlescape success
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Performance ratings displayed as superior; alien technology advancement slows or reverses; X-COM tech tree visibly advances faster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty scales upward; alien equipment in future encounters remains static or degrades; player gains strategic leverage over city politics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Performance ratings show poor results; alien technology advancement accelerates; UFO craft appear with upgraded weaponry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty decreases to allow player recovery; aliens gain technological edge; political organizations lose confidence in X-COM

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late campaign: Gateway to alien homeworld accessible
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Research completion markers show gateway access unlocked; battlescape missions shift to extradimensional environments; alien homeworld visual aesthetic distinct from Earth city
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Campaign shifts to offensive phase; X-COM can project forces through tetrahedron gates; enslaved Sectoid survivors encountered in alien dimension

environmental_storytelling.technique: Alien life cycle visualization through specimen types
environmental_storytelling.example: Players encounter Brain-Sucker creatures in lower-level alien attacks during specimen gathering missions; creature animation and behavior (latching onto heads, injecting substance, dying) communicates biological threat and alien reproductive strategy

Organizations visually occupy districts or facilities in Mega-Primus cityscape; blockade indicators show corporate power plays; control percentage displays reveal progressive alien infiltration of business entities

environmental_storytelling.technique: Tetrahedron gate architecture as alien signature
environmental_storytelling.example: Distinctive geometric portal design identifies alien intrusion points on battlefield; visual consistency across deployments establishes alien technological identity separate from human infrastructure
Sectoid-human hybrids and androids represented as distinct enclaves within Mega-Primus; visual segregation communicates social tension and political pressure group formation

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Destroyed buildings and environmental devastation from combat persist in cityscape; visual accumulation communicates X-COM's difficult balance between alien suppression and civilian protection

environmental_storytelling.example: Mega-Primus built over ruins of Toronto; contained megalopolis architecture communicates Earth's damaged biosphere and humanity's refuge strategy after T'leth battle; distinct from previous game's global scope

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Radar blips indicate movement patterns; visual trajectory displayed in cityscape; collision risk with city infrastructure implied
Jumps toward nearest individual unit; animation shows head-latching attack; death after injection communicates life cycle dependency and parasitic reproduction strategy
Battlefield success reflected in weekly performance metrics; influence on alien tech advancement visible in future encounter preparation
Provides funding or resource access; independent manufacturing and profit operations; covert battles with rival organizations
Withdraws support; blocks X-COM personnel movement; actively attacks X-COM forces; demands compensation; visually shown as controlled entity on cityscape
Inherently hostile engagement; coordinate with or worship alien invaders; actively undermine X-COM objectives
Different races show variable strengths/weaknesses; interdependence between races influences tactical deployment; technology advancement visible in equipment upgrades across sequential encounters

notes: X-COM: Apocalypse represents a significant shift in world-state communication compared to prior X-COM titles. The singular Mega-Primus setting allows fine-grained visual tracking of political faction relationships, alien infestation progress, and collateral damage consequences. The self-learning AI difficulty system creates emergent visual feedback: player performance directly manifests as accelerated or retarded alien technological advancement, visible through equipment progression in battlescape. The tetrahedron gate serves as a iconic alien presence marker distinct from the sectoid-focused threat of earlier games. Environmental storytelling emphasizes the post-apocalyptic context (Earth's biosphere damaged, humanity in enclosed megacities) and introduces moral complexity through hybrid populations and corporate factionalism—visual elements absent from first two X-COM titles. The isometric battlescape combined with real-time option creates dual perception modes: management mode emphasizes political/financial threat signaling, while battlescape emphasizes tactical alien variety and life-cycle mechanics.
